title: capsella bursa-pastoris aestivalis
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris aestivalis, also known as the wild cress or land cress, is a summer annual plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is native to Europe and Western Asia but has been naturalized in many parts of the world. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and other respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, are commonly used to soothe sore throats and promote digestive health, and it is also used as a food ingredient in salads and as a garnish.
